title: Crispy Sprout Quesadillas
summary: Sprouts aren’t just for Christmas. These quesadillas are a great way to use up sprouts during the festive season and beyond. They’re smoky, spicy and very cheesy. We've used flour tortillas but you can use corn if you can find them.

method.description: Finely shred your brussel sprouts and get them in a large roasting dish. Sprinkle over your sweet smoked paprika, ground coriander and season generously with salt. Pour over a generous glug of olive oil and give it a really good toss so all the ingredients are combined.
Get your roasting dish in the oven and cook for 20 mins, tossing every 5 mins so that it cooks evenly.

Make your salsa. Roughly chop your garlic clove and green chilli (removing the seeds first), then pop them in a blender. Add your coriander (leaves and stalks), the juice of your limes and a glug of olive oil, then whizz it to a smooth sauce. Season it to taste with salt.
Grate your mozzarella and Cheddar, and mix them together in a bowl. Sprinkle the cheese onto one half of your flour tortillas, then fold them back over.

Heat a large frying pan over a low heat, and place a few tortillas in there. Cook them for about 3 mins on each side, until the cheese is melted. Transfer to a plate and cover with a tea towel to keep them warm whilst you finish the rest.
Pull your quesadillas open and fill them with the crispy sprout mixture. Drizzle over your coriander salsa, then serve them up.

chefs.name: Sophie Wyburd
chefs.summary:
Sophie Wyburd is a cook and food writer specialising in simple, hearty comfort food, informed by her love of cookbooks and background in restaurant kitchens. You can find her co-hosting the 'A Bit of a Mouthful' and 'I’ll Have What She’s Having' podcasts, and hosting sell-out supper clubs in London. Her debut cookbook 'Tucking In' will be released in June 2024.
